reasoning: The document has a very high LLM fidelity composite score (0.9973) but triggered 3 major programmatic defects. These defects indicate that the document is a scanned PDF where the entire page image was included alongside the extracted OCR text, causing severe content duplication for screen readers. According to the count-based routing thresholds, the presence of 3 major defects mandates a HUMAN_REVIEW.

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Scanned document: source has 0 extractable text but output contains 322 words (OCR'd from images)
concerns.analysis: baseline_words=0, gemini_words=322. Entire document is image-based; output likely duplicates content as both page image and extracted text.

concerns.description: Output has more images than source PDF (ratio 3.0x) — possible image duplication
concerns.analysis: image_count_ratio=3.00. More images in output than in source indicates duplicated or fabricated image elements.

The source document includes a sentence that is underlined for emphasis. In the extracted JSON, this sentence is presented as plain text without any indication of underlining, resulting in visual emphasis loss as outlined in the evaluation rules.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Please attach this letter to the cover of your existing mining permit for future reference.
concerns.output_quote: Please attach this letter to the cover of your existing mining permit for future reference.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (1): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality | Divisio
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

action_items: Remove or mark as decorative the full-page images that duplicate the extracted text
Strip any retained headers or footers from the text flow
Apply appropriate HTML formatting (e.g., <u> or <em>) for the missing visual emphasis
